task: Drag the plate back after holding it down
raw:
SCENE ANALYSIS - A small, light-colored plate is on a brown cardboard surface. The plate is rigid and flat. STRATEGY - First, reach for the plate to establish contact. Then, grasp it to lift it. Next, transport it backward (away from the cardboard's edge) and place it down. Failure modes include dropping the plate during transport or placing it in an unstable position. AFFORDANCE REASONING - Step 1 reach the plate at its body for initial contact. Step 2 grasp the plate's body for a secure hold. Step 3 transport the plate backward over the cardboard. Step 4 place the plate on the cardboard to complete the task.
Goal: on(plate, cardboard); stable(plate)
Scene: plate, cardboard
Step 1: reach(plate)
  affordance_region: the body of the plate
  contact: gripper_state(open) [progress]
  spatial: distance(gripper, plate, <, 0.03) [completion]
  direction: approach_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: distance(gripper, plate) < 0.03
Step 2: grasp(plate)
  affordance_region: the body of the plate
  contact: holding(plate) [completion]
  direction: grasp_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: holding(plate)
Step 3: transport(plate -> cardboard)
  affordance_region: an empty area on the cardboard behind the plate
  spatial: above(plate, cardboard, 0.05) [completion]
  safety: no_drop(plate)
  done_when: above(plate, cardboard, 0.05)
Step 4: place(plate -> cardboard)
  affordance_region: an empty area on the cardboard behind the plate
  contact: surface_contact(plate, cardboard) [completion]; released(plate) [completion]
  pose: stable(plate) [completion]
  done_when: surface_contact(plate, cardboard) AND released(plate)
<END_OF_PLAN>

steps.affordance_region: an empty area on the cardboard behind the plate
an empty area on the cardboard behind the plate
